description:
GOAL: Real heap with mark-sweep GC, schema-driven generic tracer, explicit-roots API for testing. Real root tracking comes from CPS codegen later (.11.7-.11.9).

## Decisions captured

- Schemas are THE CONTRACT between codegen and GC. A Schema describes ordered fields with offsets and FieldKind ∈ {FzValue, RawBytes(n)}. Tracer dispatches on header.kind; for Struct, looks up Schema by schema_id and iterates FzValue fields.
- Single contiguous region for v1 (Box<[u8]>). mmap-backed growable later.
- Freelist-based mark-sweep, NON-MOVING. Non-moving important so .14 SIMD codegen can rely on stable Vec addresses.
- Per-kind tracer branches for List / Bitstring / VecI64/F64/U8/Bit / Map (bespoke logic). Frames, tuples, future user records all flow through Struct + generic schema-driven tracing.
- Explicit-root API: gc(roots: &[FzValue]). CPS codegen in .11.9 produces real root sets from frame chains; tests pass roots in directly for now.

## Deliverables (new src/heap.rs)

```rust
pub struct Schema {
    pub name: String,                 // debug
    pub size: u32,                    // bytes after HeapHeader
    pub fields: Vec<FieldDescriptor>,
}

pub struct FieldDescriptor {
    pub offset: u32,                  // bytes from start of payload
    pub kind: FieldKind,
}

pub enum FieldKind {
    FzValue,
    RawBytes(u32),
}

pub struct SchemaRegistry { schemas: Vec<Schema> }
impl SchemaRegistry {
    pub fn register(&mut self, schema: Schema) -> u32;
    pub fn get(&self, id: u32) -> &Schema;
}

pub struct Heap {
    region: Box<[u8]>,
    bump: usize,
    freelist: Vec<(usize, usize)>,    // (offset, size)
    capacity: usize,
    schemas: Rc<RefCell<SchemaRegistry>>,
}

impl Heap {
    pub fn new(capacity: usize, schemas: Rc<RefCell<SchemaRegistry>>) -> Self;
    pub fn alloc(&mut self, size: usize) -> *mut HeapHeader;
    pub fn gc(&mut self, roots: &[FzValue]);
}
```

## Tracer algorithm

Walks each root, marks reachable, then sweeps:

```
trace(obj, registry, mark_set):
    if mark_set.is_marked(obj): return
    mark_set.mark(obj)
    match (*obj).kind:
        Struct:
            schema = registry.get((*obj).schema_id)
            for field in schema.fields where field.kind == FzValue:
                child = read FzValue at obj + sizeof(HeapHeader) + field.offset
                if child.is_ptr(): trace(child.unbox_ptr(), registry, mark_set)
        List:
            head = read FzValue at obj+16+0
            tail = read FzValue at obj+16+8
            if head.is_ptr(): trace(head, ...)
            if tail.is_ptr(): trace(tail, ...)
        Bitstring | VecI64 | VecF64 | VecU8 | VecBit:
            // raw payloads, nothing to trace
        Map:
            walk (key, value) entries; trace each FzValue
```

Sweep walks heap linearly using header.size_bytes to advance; freed unmarked objects added to freelist.

## Tests

- Allocate N structs with various schemas; gc with all roots; all survive.
- Drop roots; gc; objects freed (freelist grows or bump rewinds).
- Reachability through nested structs (parent struct field points to child struct).
- Reachability through cons cells.
- Cycle (A → B → A) kept alive by either root, freed when both lost.
- Vec(I64) freed properly when unrooted.

## Open / research

- Bump-only with full-heap mark-sweep simplest for v1; freelist first-fit when fragmentation appears.
- GC trigger threshold (75% full? manual gc() for tests v1).

## Out of scope

- JIT root scanning (.11.9 bridges)
- Generational nursery (follow-up)
- Concurrent / incremental GC (follow-up)
- mmap-backed growable heap (follow-up)
title: Schema registry + heap allocator + mark-sweep STW
